revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:布怒豆怒神]]


{{Infobox deity
| type = Shinto
| children = {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}}
| gender = {{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}
| offspring = {{ill|Futemimi|qid=Q65266228|1=Futemimi}}
}}
'''Funozuno''' (布怒豆怒神, Master Twisted Vines) was a Japanese deity<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Funozuno • . A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |url=https://historyofjapan.co.uk/wiki/funozuno/ |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=. A History . . of Japan . 日本歴史 |language=en-GB}}</ref>. He is the father of {{ill|Futemimi|ja|布帝耳神|1=Futemimi|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Futemimi|qq=}} and his vines were bleached and made into clothing.<ref name=":0" /> He is considered a descendant of [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] (Raging Man).<ref>{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=HwMZBQAAQBAJ&dq=Funozuno&pg=PA229 |title=The Kojiki: An Account of Ancient Matters |date=2014-09-30 |publisher=Columbia University Press |isbn=978-0-231-16389-7 |pages=229 |language=en}}</ref> He was the grandfather of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Fuyukinu|qq=}}<ref>Philippi, Donald L. (2015). ''Kojiki''. Princeton University Press. p. 92.</ref><ref>Chamberlain (1882). [http://sacred-texts.com/shi/kj/kj027.htm Section XX.—The August Ancestors of the Deity-Master-Of-The-Great Land.]</ref><ref name="fountainhead2" />{{rp|278|qq=draft}} and great grandfather of [[Ōkuninushi]].<ref name="fountainhead2">{{cite book |last=Herbert |first=J. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&pg=PT402 |title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan |publisher=Taylor & Francis |year=2010 |isbn=978-1-136-90376-2 |series=Routledge Library Editions: Japan |page=402 |access-date=2020-11-21}}</ref>{{rp|278}}

== Overview ==
In the [[Kojiki]], his name is written as as {{Nihongo|Funumamenu-no-kami|布怒豆怒神}}, and in the {{ill|Awaga Shrine|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awaga Shrine|qq=}} book, {{ill|Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|ja|粟鹿大明神元記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awaga Daimyojin Mototsufumi|qq=}}, he is written as {{Nihongo|Funotsu-[[Mimi and Mi|mimi]]|布努都弥美}}.

There are several theories regarding the name (名義). One theory links it to the place name 'Funogo' (布努郷) in Miyoshi District, Bingo Province. Another theory considers 'Funo' to be a phonetic transformation of 'Kuna' (曲, meaning 'curve'), while 'Zuno' (ヅノ) is interpreted as meaning {{ill|kudzu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kudzu|qq=}} (葛, a type of vine), suggesting that it refers to {{Nihongo|twisting kudzu vines creeping along|くねくねと這う葛|Kunekune to hau kuzu}}. This naming is believed to be based on materials from which fibers are extracted for clothing after being soaked in water. There's also a theory that 'Nunozuno' (ヌノヅノ), originating from 'Nunozuna' (布綱, meaning 'fabric rope'), was mistakenly transcribed and established as a divine name in the 'Kojiki' (古事記). This theory sees the name as the deification of a fabric rope. <ref>國學院大学　古事記学センター　布怒豆怒神</ref>

==Family tree==
{{Eight generations of Izumo}}
